info.name: Monari [TEST]
info.description: OpenAPI documentation for the external systems interface. ## Explanation of the description notation: In the description, the designations **POST\[\], PATCH\[\]** and **GET\[\]** can be found in a uniform structure. The \[X\] in the square brackets means that this field is used in **POST/PATCH** or **GET**, depending on where the X was set. If it is a _readOnly_ field, then an **X** is set in the **GET** and is not used in **POST** or/and **PATCH**. The default is when **POST** and **PATCH** are _writeOnly_ (i.e. the field is used in both). In this case, the description is left empty. Structure for link fields: **Link field:** _Alias, UUID_ | **<**Where to link to(name)**\>** It is linked via an _Alias_ or a _UUID_. Contact Support: Email: [info@system-provider.com](https://mailto:info@system-provider.com)

item.name: OrdersNotification (Incoming)
Update an existing order [all min fields]
Receive the current information from an specific order sub status.

request.description:
<u>__query parameters that can be used to filter__</u> 
  - all status=[]
  - site__alias=[]
  - order_class__alias=[]

  To request a list the values need to be separated by a comma like in the example below.

<u>__Example__</u> 
 ```
 .../?site__alias=<site-alias>&order_class__alias=product-outgoing-order-class&loading_status=00,40
 ```

item.name: Handling Unit notification from external system.
Request handling unit head & position information

request.description:
<u>__query parameters that can be used to filter__</u> 
 - site__alias=[]
 - handling_unit_class__alias=[]

 To request a list the values need to be separated by a comma like in the example below.

 <u>__Example__</u> 
 ```
 .../?site__alias=<site-alias>&handling_unit_class__alias=stock-handling-unit
 ```
